Stellantis, which is the parent company of Jeep, Chrysler, Dodge, Ram, Fiat, Peugeot, … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Andrew Liszewski https://www.theverge.com/?p=966560 2026-07-21T10:06:44-04:00 2026-07-21T10:00:00-04:00 If you’ve been undecided on which Xteink device to get, the new X4 Pro is it. The familiar story with Xteink’s tiny e-readers plays out once again with its new X4 Pro: upgraded hardware held back by frustrating software. The company’s latest tiny e-reader adds a touchscreen and lighting, so choosing it over a Kindle or a Kobo feels like less of a compromise — until you use it. The new touch controls and some welcome software updates do manage to make the X4 Pro easier to use, but frustrations still abound. There’s a happier ending to the story this time, however, as the team behind the excellent CrossPoint Reader software plan to have their alternative firmware ready for the new device at its launch. If you’ve been considering one of Xteink’s e-readers, the X4 Pro is the one to get, even if it’s the most expensive yet. The X4 Pro is available for purchase today through Xteink’s online store for $99, making it slightly more expensive than the company’s $79 X3 and $69 X4 models . That also brings the X4 Pro closer in price to the $110 entry-level Kindle featuring Amazon’s polished e-reader experience and on-device access to a well-stocked digital bookstore. The X4 Pro lacks both of those things, but with a 4.26-inch, 800 x 480 E Ink screen, it’s another extremely pocketable device from Xteink that’s slightly smaller and thinner than the company’s first e-reader, and considerably smaller than Amazon’s 6-inch Kindle. That thin profile means you’ll need to be prepared for a big compromise: There’s no longer an actual USB-C port. You can still benefit from USB-C functionality but will need an adapter to deal with an uncommon magnetic pogo-pin connection. The adapter is included, but I’d still opt for a slightly thicker device with an actual USB-C port. On the back of the X4 Pro you’ll once again find embedded magnets so you can attach it to compatible smartphones like a wireless battery. The magnets feel stronger, but still not strong enough to make me confident the e-reader is going to remain stuck to my phone while jostling around in my pocket. The X4 Pro’s size also makes the tiny e-reader awkwardly extend past the bottom of my iPhone 16 Pro when attached, and at this point I think Xteink should just abandon promoting this functionality. Its e-readers are better as standalone devices. The added touchscreen allows for a much simpler button layout on the X4 Pro, and I’m glad the company still includes dedicated page turn buttons on each side in addition to being able to tap the edges of the screen. There’s also a power/wake button and, for the first time, a touch button on the front of the e-reader below its screen for jumping back to the e-reader’s homescreen.  There’s been some debate about whether or not these tiny e-readers actually need a touchscreen, but after testing the X4 Pro for over a week I think it’s a welcome upgrade for these devices. Even after months of use, navigating the company’s other e-readers using just buttons has never felt intuitive or easy, and it’s held me back from wanting to carry them everywhere I go. Navigating the X4 Pro using screen taps (like we do with the rest of our mobile devices) finally makes it feel like I’m not fighting the e-reader to make it do what I want. But the X4 Pro’s touchscreen doesn’t feel as sensitive or responsive as a Kindle or Kobo when tapping on the screen, and half the time it feels like I have to repeat a tap with a little more pressure for my finger to be detected. I’m hoping that’s something that can be improved through future software updates. The added screen light is an excellent upgrade, and even though it’s a bit uneven and definitely a little brighter at the top of the display, you can adjust its intensity and color temperature using sliders that appear when you swipe down from the top of the screen. There are no automatic adjustments or scheduling available yet, but being able to finally use this e-reader in bed without having a lamp on is a game changer, and I can’t see myself going back to Xteink’s older devices that lack lighting. Xteink has made a lot of improvements to its bundled software and UI to accommodate the X4 Pro’s touchscreen. The menus have been streamlined and are easier to navigate with touch, and accessing reading settings by tapping anywhere on the page now feels more intuitive. There’s also more flexibility with adjusting text spacing and layout, but out of the box you’re once again limited to just a single font in two different sizes unless you want to jump through hoops to install your own. But you don’t have to suffer with Xteink’s unpolished software. Xteink X4 Pro e-reader Where to Buy: $99 at Xteink Of all the X4 Pro’s improvements, the most compelling reason to get Xteink’s latest e-reader is that the easy-to-install CrossPoint Reader software will be available at launch. It’s a free firmware alternative that vastly improves the UI and reading experience of Xteink’s devices. When I tested Xteink’s last e-reader the team behind CrossPoint Reader wasn’t sure whe

oldContent: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 LawnStarter: Director of Product Management Anywhere in the World Product Full-Time <img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/5888/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" /> <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> United States <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="http://lawnstarter.com">http://lawnstarter.com</a> </p> <p><strong>About LawnStarter</strong></p> <p>LawnStarter is the nation's leading on-demand marketplace for lawn care and outdoor services, with over $100M in annual bookings. We operate across three brands (LawnStarter, Lawn Love, Home Gnome) on a single shared platform, and we've been profitable for two years running. We're expanding beyond lawn care to become the one-stop shop for all home services.</p> <p><strong>About Product at LawnStarter</strong></p> <p>Home services is a massive, broken market. For homeowners, getting reliable, fairly priced service is a hassle of phone tag, no-shows, and surprise quotes. For the Pros who do the work, running a business means chasing customers, dead time between jobs, and unpaid admin. LawnStarter is the marketplace that fixes both sides.</p> <p>We proved the model with lawn care — it works at scale, profitably, across millions of jobs. In the last year we extended it to a second service: pool cleaning, now live in select markets. Getting there meant building the first version of a product that can onboard customers and Pros, match the two sides, get the job done, and handle the exceptions when it doesn't go to plan.</p> <p></p> <p><strong>The Role</strong></p> <p>This is LawnStarter's first Director of Product Management — the leader between the VP of Product and a team of three PMs (and the hires that follow).</p> <p>The mandate is simple to state and hard to do: ship value across&nbsp;<strong>growth, retention, and profitability</strong>&nbsp;by building a great product for customers and Pros. Concretely, that means taking us from one profitable-at-scale vertical to two, setting the path from two to&nbsp;<em>n</em>, while still aggressively improving our core lawn service.</p> <p></p> <p><strong>What Makes This Role Different</strong></p> <ul> <li> <strong>You build the layer, not inherit it.</strong>&nbsp;This is a brand-new layer between the VP and the PMs. You define how the team works, the bar for the craft, and the operating model — you're not slotting into someone else's machine.</li> <li> <strong>It's a marketplace, so the obvious answer is usually wrong.</strong>&nbsp;Change a price and you move conversion, Pro claim rates, margin, and retention all at once. Almost every decision has second-order effects across customers, Pros, and the business. You think in systems, not features.</li> <li> <strong>You multiply a strong team — this isn't a turnaround.</strong>&nbsp;The PMs are already good. The job is to get extraordinary outcomes from extraordinary people while still doing the hardest work yourself. Player-coach, not figurehead.</li> <li> <strong>Strategic bets and patient optimization, both.</strong>&nbsp;You'll open new verticals&nbsp;<em>and</em>&nbsp;compound a few points of conversion at a time — and know which the moment calls for.</li> </ul> <p></p> <p><strong>What You'll Own</strong></p> <ul> <li> <strong>The PM team</strong>: Lead, coach, and multiply three already-strong PMs — and hire more as we grow.</li> <li> <strong>Prioritization</strong>: Own the growth/retention/profitability tradeoffs on one shared roadmap — what gets built, what waits, what we kill.</li> <li> <strong>Multi-service strategy</strong>: Turn one proven vertical into a repeatable playbook across services, designing for customers and Pros as one system.</li> <li> <strong>Agent-native direction</strong>: Own how AI and agents show up in what we build — for our team, our customers, and our Pros.</li> <li> <strong>Cross-functional partnership</strong>: Be the product leader the rest of the company trusts to make the right calls.</li> </ul> <p></p> <p><strong>Problems to Solve</strong></p> <p><strong>Abstracting one product into a multi-service platform.</strong>&nbsp;Every service has different economics, quality signals, and Pro workflows. How do we judge Pro quality when services are nothing alike? Price any service by location and job complexity? Guarantee quality on every job, not just lawns? You'll decide what generalizes and what stays service-specific, then sequence the build to a second profitable vertical — a platform that absorbs that variation instead of forking under it.</p> <p><strong>Prioritizing across three goals with one capacity.</strong>&nbsp;Growth, retention, and profitability all want the roadmap, and engineering capacity is finite — a win on one can cost you on another. You'll own the sequencing and tradeoffs, and say no clearly and often, with reasons people respect.</p> <p><strong>Building for the agent era.</strong>&nbsp;We believe products must now be built to&nbsp;<em>use</em>&nbsp;agents (to move faster and serve customers and Pros better) and to&nbsp;<em>serve</em>&nbsp;agents (so the platform works when the buyer or the worker is an AI). Almost no one has a playbook for this — you'll help write ours.</p> <p></p> <p><strong>What Success Looks Like (Year 1)</strong></p> <ul> <li> <strong>The team ships at a higher ceiling</strong>: The same strong PMs are visibly doing their best work — better outcomes, sharper scoping, faster shipping.</li> <li> <strong>Wins in all three goals</strong>: Measurable progress on growth, retention,&nbsp;<em>and</em>&nbsp;profitability — not one at the expense of the others.</li> <li> <strong>Second vertical on track</strong>: Pool cleaning progressing toward profitable-at-scale, with a repeatable playbook for the verticals after it taking shape.</li> <li> <strong>A stronger brand through quality</strong>: Measurable improvement in the quality of our core service, and a clear plan with the growth team to win AI-driven channels.</li> <li> <strong>An agent-native vision</strong>: A clear roadmap for winning in an agent-driven world, with the first bets already underway.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Requirements</strong></p> <p><strong>Who You Are</strong></p> <p><strong>AI-native and agent-curious.</strong>&nbsp;You use AI every day and push the team to do the same. You believe agents are the next platform shift, and you're energized by the open question of what an agent-driven world means for LawnStarter — a genuine thought partner on it, not someone who shows up with all the answers. This is unlikely to be a good fit if you treat AI as a productivity hack.</p> <p><strong>A player-coach who multiplies talent.</strong>&nbsp;You get extraordinary outcomes from already-strong people — coaching,
newContent: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 Sun Life: Iowa Clinical Investigator Consultant - Must Reside in Iowa Iowa Management and Finance Full-Time <img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/5910/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" /> <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> U.S. Employees (Remote) <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="http://sunlife.com">http://sunlife.com</a> </p> <div><p>Sun Life U.S. is one of the largest providers of employee and government benefits, helping approximately 50 million Americans access the care and coverage they need. Through employers, industry partners and government programs, Sun Life U.S. offers a portfolio of benefits and services, including dental, vision, disability, absence management, life, supplemental health, medical stop-loss insurance, and healthcare navigation. We have more than 6,400 employees and associates in our partner dental practices and operate nationwide.</p><p></p><p><a href="https://www.sunlife.com/us/en/">Visit our website to discover how Sun Life is making life brighter for our customers, partners and communities. </a>&nbsp;</p><p></p><p><i><span>DentaQuest manages dental and vision benefits for more than 33 million Americans. Our outcomes-based, cost-effective solutions are designed for Medicaid and CHIP, Medicare Advantage, small and large businesses, and individuals. With a focus on prevention and value, we aim to make quality care accessible to improve the oral health of all.</span></i></p><div><div><div><div><div><div><div><p></p>&nbsp;<br><p><b>Job Description: </b></p></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><p><span><span>Sun Life embraces a hybrid work model that balances in-office </span></span><span><span>collaboration with the flexibility of virtual work in the contiguous states </span></span><span><span>plus AK. </span></span></p><p></p><p><b>** Candidate must reside in the state of Iowa. </b></p><p></p><p><b>The Opportunity: </b></p><p>The Clinical Investigator is responsible for the retrospective review of paid claims and analysis of claims data to identify provider fraud, waste or abuse. The Clinical Investigator will conduct in-depth analysis, peer analysis trend analysis and statistical analysis to identify providers whose utilization patterns are outside of the expected norm from their peers in their assigned markets. They will have full responsibility for all contractual obligations for their assigned clients related to fraud, waste or abuse. The Clinical Investigator is responsible for the professional relationship with assigned clients related to fraud, waste and abuse and is responsible for all aspects of the investigative process from identification, initiation, completion and referral of the investigation as applicable. The Clinical Investigator is responsible for working with providers to educate, impose corrective action and to reach case resolution. The Investigator works closely with clients, managed care organizations, state agencies and law enforcement related to their investigations as required.</p><p></p><p><b><span>How you will contribute:</span></b><span><span> </span></span><span>&nbsp;</span></p><ul><li><p>Responsible for clinical interpretation of dental radiographs.</p></li><li><p>Independently interpret utilization reports to detect patterns and potential areas of fraud, waste or abuse.</p></li><li><p>Query data to conduct comprehensive drill down data analysis to detect patterns and trends of under and over utilization.</p></li><li><p>Investigate provider and member fraud and abuse in assigned markets.</p></li><li><p>Manage multiple clients and associated provider networks related to fraud, waste and abuse investigation.</p></li><li><p>Work with and in consultation with DentaQuest Dental Directors during the investigative process to assure accurate and defensible clinical decisions.</p></li><li><p>Responsible for the accuracy of all investigative decisions and that the decisions are clinically sound,&nbsp; supported by appropriate and documented authority and defensible.</p></li><li><p>Assure all audit decisions are in compliance with Medicare regulations, State Medicaid Guidelines, DentaQuest Office Reference Manual and plan benefit limitations.</p></li><li><p>Assist management in overpayment negotiations with providers related to the results and findings of an investigation.</p></li><li><p>Responsible for the implementation and updating of all client processes related to Fraud, Waste and Abuse.</p></li><li><p>Maintain the professional relationship with assigned client contacts related to fraud, waste and abuse investigation.</p></li><li><p>Responsible for all investigation and client related communications to providers for provision of investigative facts and findings.</p></li><li><p>Responsible for all client facing meetings related to fraud, waste and abuse.</p></li><li><p>Track all investigative cases in accurate timeline format in internal fraud tracking database.</p></li><li><p>Have the ability to independently manage workflow to meet business needs, assure contractual obligations for all clients are met and to meet departmental metrics.</p></li><li><p>Responsible for the development of interdepartmental relationships to assure all issues related to fraud, waste or abuse are identified and appropriately investigated.</p></li><li><p>Responsible for the preparation and accuracy of recovery documents for processing and assure that they are completed correctly and in a timely fashion.</p></li><li><p>Responsible for all assigned client contractual reporting requirements related to investigative activity and outcomes.</p></li><li><p>Maintain professional relationships with market assigned, state Medicaid agencies, and state and federal law enforcement agencies.</p></li><li><p>Assist in the ongoing evaluation of departmental policies and procedures.</p></li></ul><p></p><p><span><span> </span></span><b><span>What you will bring with you:</span></b><span><span> </span></span><span>&nbsp;</span></p><ul><li><p>Licensed Registered Dental Hygienist is preferred.</p></li><li><p>3 + years clinical experience and/or related investigative business experience.</p></li><li><p>Experience in Dental Utilization Management or Review, preferred</p></li></ul><p>Preferred skills</p><ul><li><p>Knowledge of general computer software (Excel, Word, Outlook, Adobe).</p></li><li><p>Clinical experience and knowledge of dental procedures and terminology is required.</p></li>&l

As part of its Black Friday in July sale, Best Buy has brought the Kindle Paperwhite back down to its all-time low of $124.99 ($35 off) . It’s the Kindle we recommend for most people because it has a bunch of features. It’s waterproof and features a sharp 300ppi, 7-inch display, it offers an adjustable warm light for reading at night, and it has great battery life that can last for more than a month on a single charge. Kindle Paperwhite (12th-gen) Where to Buy: $159.99 $124.99 at Best Buy (with ads) $159.99 $124.99 at Target (with ads) Best Buy has also discounted nearly every other Kindle model. The Kindle Kids is a good deal even for adults. It’s down to $99.99 ($30 off) , and includes the same hardware as the standard Kindle, but with some extras: a kid-friendly cover, no ads, a two-year worry-free guarantee, and six months of Amazon Kids Plus. If you read comics, the Kindle Colorsoft is also on sale starting at $159.99 ($90 off) . It shares many of the Paperwhite’s strengths but adds a color E Ink display that displays color at up to 150ppi. The Kindle Colorsoft Signature Edition , which has 32GB of storage and wireless charging, is on sale for $219.99 ($60 off) . If you want an e-reader that doubles as a digital notebook, the Kindle Scribe and Kindle Scribe Colorsoft are worth considering. They’re on sale starting at $379.99 ($120 off)   and $479.99 ($150 off) , respectively. Each offers an 11-inch display with an included stylus for writing notes, annotating Kindle books, and marking documents. They also include notebook templates and AI-powered tools that can summarize notes or clean up messy handwriting. The biggest difference is that the Scribe Colorsoft adds a color E Ink display, making it a better choice for reviewing color documents or organizing notes with different colors. Read our reviews of the Kindle Paperwhite , Kindle Colorsoft Signature Edition , and Kindle Scribe Colorsoft .  Kindle Kids (2024) Amazon’s Kindle Kids is identical to the standard Kindle — meaning it packs a 300ppi display and support for USB-C charging — but comes with a case, an extended two-year warranty, and six months of Amazon Kids Plus. Where to Buy: $129.99 $99.99 at Best Buy $129.99 $99.99 at Target Kindle Colorsoft (16GB) Amazon gave its Kindle lineup a much-needed jolt with the introduction of the Kindle Colorsoft last year, its first color E Ink ebook reader. Now it’s back with a cheaper 16GB model that offers many of the same perks, including up to eight weeks of battery life and a 7-inch display that’s great for comics and graphic novels. Where to Buy: $249.99 $189.99 at Amazon $249.99 $189.99 at Best Buy $249.99 at Target Kindle Scribe 32GB The third-gen Kindle Scribe 32GB is even bigger than its 10.2-inch predecessor; it offers an 11-inch display with 300ppi resolution and the same great battery life that Kindles are known for. Where to Buy: $499.99 $379.99 at Best Buy Kindle Scribe Colorsoft The Kindle Scribe Colorsoft is Amazon’s first note-taking e-reader with a color screen. It features an 11-inch display, a slimmer design, an improved interface, an updated stylus, and a new AI-powered search feature that can quickly summarize documents. Through employers, industry partners and government programs, Sun Life U.S. offers a portfolio of benefits and services, including dental, vision, disability, absence management, life, supplemental health, medical stop-loss insurance, and healthcare navigation. We have more than 6,400 employees and associates in our partner dental practices and operate nationwide.</p><p></p><p><a href="https://www.sunlife.com/us/en/">Visit our website to discover how Sun Life is making life brighter for our customers, partners and communities. </a>&nbsp;</p><p></p><p><i><span>DentaQuest manages dental and vision benefits for more than 33 million Americans. Our outcomes-based, cost-effective solutions are designed for Medicaid and CHIP, Medicare Advantage, small and large businesses, and individuals. With a focus on prevention and value, we aim to make quality care accessible to improve the oral health of all.</span></i></p><div><div><div><div><div><div><div><p></p>&nbsp;<br><p><b>Job Description: </b></p></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><p><span><span>Sun Life embraces a hybrid work model that balances in-office </span></span><span><span>collaboration with the flexibility of virtual work in the contiguous states </span></span><span><span>plus AK. </span></span></p><p></p><p><b>** Candidate must reside in the state of Iowa. </b></p><p></p><p><b>The Opportunity: </b></p><p>The Clinical Investigator is responsible for the retrospective review of paid claims and analysis of claims data to identify provider fraud, waste or abuse. The Clinical Investigator will conduct in-depth analysis, peer analysis trend analysis and statistical analysis to identify providers whose utilization patterns are outside of the expected norm from their peers in their assigned markets. They will have full responsibility for all contractual obligations for their assigned clients related to fraud, waste or abuse. The Clinical Investigator is responsible for the professional relationship with assigned clients related to fraud, waste and abuse and is responsible for all aspects of the investigative process from identification, initiation, completion and referral of the investigation as applicable. The Clinical Investigator is responsible for working with providers to educate, impose corrective action and to reach case resolution. The Investigator works closely with clients, managed care organizations, state agencies and law enforcement related to their investigations as required.</p><p></p><p><b><span>How you will contribute:</span></b><span><span> </span></span><span>&nbsp;</span></p><ul><li><p>Responsible for clinical interpretation of dental radiographs.</p></li><li><p>Independently interpret utilization reports to detect patterns and potential areas of fraud, waste or abuse.</p></li><li><p>Query data to conduct comprehensive drill down data analysis to detect patterns and trends of under and over utilization.</p></li><li><p>Investigate provider and member fraud and abuse in assigned markets.</p></li><li><p>Manage multiple clients and associated provider networks related to fraud, waste and abuse investigation.</p></li><li><p>Work with and in consultation with DentaQuest Dental Directors during the investigative process to assure accurate and defensible clinical decisions.</p></li><li><p>Responsible for the accuracy of all investigative decisions and that the decisions are clinically sound,&nbsp; supported by appropriate and documented authority and defensible.</p></li><li><p>Assure all audit decisions are in compliance with Medicare regulations, State Medicaid Guidelines, DentaQuest Office Reference Manual and plan benefit limitations.</p></li><li><p>Assist management in overpayment negotiations with providers related to the results and findings of an investigation.</p></li><li><p>Responsible for the implementation and updating of all client processes related to Fraud, Waste and Abuse.</p></li><li><p>Maintain the professional relationship with assigned client contacts related to fraud, waste and abuse investigation.</p></li><li><p>Responsible for all investigation and client related communications to providers for provision of investigative facts and findings.</p></li><li><p>Responsible for all client facing meetings related to fraud, waste and abuse.</p></li><li><p>Track all investigative cases in accurate timeline format in internal fraud tracking database.</p></li><li><p>Have the ability to independently manage workflow to meet business needs, assure contractual obligations for all clients are met and to meet departmental metrics.</p></li><li><p>Responsible for the development of interdepartmental relationships to assure all issues related to fraud, waste or abuse are identified and appropriately investigated.</p></li><li><p>Responsible for the preparation and accuracy of recovery documents for processing and assure that they are completed correctly and in a timely fashion.</p></li><li><p>Responsible for all assigned client contractual reporting requirements related to investigative activity and outcomes.</p></li><li><p>Maintain professional relationships with market assigned, state Medicaid agencies, and state and federal law enforcement agencies.</p></li><li><p>Assist in the ongoing evaluation of departmental policies and procedures.</p></li></ul><p></p><p><span><span> </span></span><b><span>What you will bring with business experience.</p></li><li><p>Experience in Dental Utilization Management or Review, preferred</p></li></ul><p>Preferred skills</p><ul><li><p>Knowledge of general computer software (Excel, Word, newContent: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 Center for Food Safety: Staff Attorney Anywhere in the World California Writing, Legal, and Legal Research All Other Remote Full-Time <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> San Francisco <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://www.centerforfoodsafety.org/">https://www.centerforfoodsafety.org/</a> </p> <h2 class="css-of4wst">Staff Attorney</h2> <div class="css-182q8w0"> <div class="css-1nb1zny"> <div class=""> <div class="ATS_htmlPreview"> <p><strong>Location: The position is preferred to be based in either Portland, Oregon, or the San Francisco Bay Area, California, in a hybrid home-office model, but fully remote is also considered for the right candidate.</strong><br><br></p> <h4><strong>TO APPLY FOR THIS ROLE, PLEASE VISIT THE </strong><a class="css-173makr-linkStyle" href="https://ats.rippling.com/center-for-food-safety/jobs"><strong>CENTER FOR FOOD SAFETY JOB BOARD</strong></a><br><br><strong>W</strong><strong>ho we are</strong></h4> <p>Center for Food Safety (CFS)'s mission is to empower people, support farmers, and protect the Earth from the harmful impacts of industrial agriculture. We defend and promote your right to safe food and a clean environment through groundbreaking legal, scientific, and grassroots action.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>For over 25 years, we have created a new food future, fostering a more powerful food movement against the harmful industrial agricultural system while promoting organic, ecological, and regenerative alternatives. We've won high-profile lawsuits to protect public health and endangered species from pesticides like Monsanto’s Roundup and dicamba. We prevented the introduction of genetically engineered wheat, rice, potatoes, and more to the market and secured meaningful labeling to ensure that we all know what's in our food. We succeeded in embedding animal welfare requirements in the organic standards and protecting the oceans from industrial aquaculture. And much more: Our groundbreaking legal cases that protect pollinators, farmworkers, and farming communities, and the earth from the devastating impacts of industrial farming, are a landmark body of case law on food and much more: Our groundbreaking legal cases that protect pollinators, farmworkers, and farming communities, and the earth from the devastating impacts of industrial farming, are a landmark body of case law on food and agricultural issues, transforming our food system.</p> <p><strong>About the role</strong></p> <p><a class="css-173makr-linkStyle" href="http://www.centerforfoodsafety.org/">Center for Food Safety</a>&nbsp;(CFS), one of the nation’s top food and environmental nonprofits, seeks a new <strong>Staff Attorney</strong> to join our team. The Staff Attorney should have at least four years of litigation experience, including clerkships and/or fellowships. CFS Staff Attorneys develop and prosecute precedent-setting cases in furtherance of CFS’s organizational mission and theory of change. The Attorney will participate in and, as appropriate, lead all phases of public interest high-impact litigation and administrative advocacy. CFS Staff Attorneys work with clients, allies, frontline communities, coalitions, and collaborative networks to apply and implement CFS campaigns. Staff Attorneys engage in public speaking and outreach. The Attorney will work on all CFS program areas, including but not limited to:</p> <ul> <li>Consumer Advocacy</li> <li>Farmworker and Farmer Protection</li> <li>Food and Climate Change</li> <li>Food Labeling/Right to Know</li> <li>Genetically Engineered Organisms and Monocultures</li> <li>Industrial Animal Agriculture</li> <li>Industrial Aquaculture</li> <li>Pesticides &amp; Other Toxins</li> <li>Pollinator &amp; Wildlife Protection</li> <li>Public Health</li> <li>Regenerative Agriculture: Organic &amp; Beyond</li> </ul> <p><strong>What you'll do</strong></p> <ul> <li>Support litigation and advocacy developed by CFS’s Legal Team and Legal Director.</li> <li>Serve as lead counsel or co-counsel as appropriate in CFS cases.</li> <li>Fulfill the full range of litigation tasks, including factual investigation, legal research and analysis, discovery and record review, briefing, oral argument, appeals, amicus briefs, settlement, and fee recovery.</li> <li>Lead the factual development for and litigation of precedent-setting public interest cases, furthering CFS’s theory of change.</li> <li>Represent CFS and clients in state and federal rulemaking and agency engagement.</li> <li>Support and engage in federal and state legislative activities connected to CFS litigation and organizational mission.</li> <li>Work closely with other CFS attorneys, policy and communications staff, scientists, coalition partners, and clients to build cases and support CFS policies and programs.</li> <li>Engage in public speaking, media, and outreach.</li> <li>Maintain and develop organizational partnerships with new clients, allies, and coalition partners to implement CFS's strategic litigation and organizational mission.</li> <li>Oversee fellows and law clerks and engage in recruitment efforts as appropriate.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Desired qualifications and skills</strong></p> <ul> <li>Law school graduate with at least 4 years of litigation experience, including clerkships or fellowships (administrative/environmental/animal law or related area preferred);</li> <li>Ability to independently manage a litigation docket.</li> <li>Excellent analytical, legal research, and writing skills.</li> <li>Excellent communication and public speaking skills.</li> <li>Oral argument experience.</li> <li>Self-motivation and willingness to do administrative tasks.</li> <li>Proven ability to work collaboratively with others in a ,small, team-oriented environment.</li> <li>Strong work ethic, initiative, and creativity.</li> <li>Sensitivity to the concerns of and the ability to work with individuals from diverse cultures, backgrounds, and orientations.</li> <li>Admission to the California or Oregon bar or a willingness to be licensed in either state within one year (or bar admission in an existing remote location).</li> <li>A passion for enforcing and strengthening environmental laws.</li> <li>A passion for and/or a background in food and agriculture issues.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation and benefits</strong></p> <p>Hybrid - San Francisco, CA: $87,000-$133,000 USD/ year (salary)<br>Hybrid - Washington, D.C.: $82,000-$123,000 USD/ year (salary)</p> <p>Hybrid - Portland, OR: $77,000-$113,000 USD/ year (salary)<br><br></p> <p>CFS offers a competitive compensation package, a flexible hybrid work environment, and a four-day workweek. In addition to salary, we offer a discretionary annual bonus and employer matching contributions to a retirement plan. Benefits include medical, vision, and dental insurance, disability insurance, a pre-tax medical savings plan, and generous vacation and leave policies. As a 501(c)(3) organization, CFS
